criteria.name: controls_keyboard_accessible
criteria.description: Every interactive control — palette entries, toolbar buttons including Undo, Redo, Artifact, Delete selected, Run/Pause/Resume/Retry/Save, node disclosures, timeline filters and entries, modal fields, panel toggles, artifact format tabs, Copy, Download, and Import — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone, with a visible focus indicator
criteria.reasoning: Keyboard focus reached palette controls with a visible ring; Enter added a node, Alt+N selected a node, Enter opened configuration, and Delete removed a node. Evidence: keyboard-node.png

criteria.name: modals_manage_focus
criteria.description: Modal dialogs (configuration, save, confirmation, import) use role dialog with aria-modal true, trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to the originating control
criteria.reasoning: Observed configuration and import dialogs with role=dialog and aria-modal=true; Escape closed the configuration dialog and returned focus to the originating palette control. Evidence: form-modal.png

criteria.name: icons_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: Toolbar and status icons that convey meaning expose descriptive accessible names (aria-label or equivalent); purely decorative icons are hidden from the accessibility tree
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Multiple focusable icon-only buttons had no aria-label, title, or text, even though decorative SVGs were hidden. Evidence: accessibility-initial.png

criteria.name: feedback_uses_live_regions
criteria.description: Validation errors, run state transitions (started, retrying, failed, complete), copy confirmation, and import validation failures are conveyed both visually and via an aria-live region
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Run status and deletion toasts used live regions, but invalid import input produced empty alert elements and no validation message. Evidence: form-modal.png

criteria.name: form_fields_labeled
criteria.description: Every configuration, save, and import field has an explicit associated label; validation messages are associated with their fields and name the field key
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Configuration/import fields displayed nearby text but had no associated label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby; label elements were empty. Evidence: form-modal.png

criteria.name: heading_and_landmark_structure
criteria.description: Panel titles (Palette, Saved Workflows, Timeline, Artifact) and main regions use a logical heading order and landmark roles so the workspace is navigable by structure
criteria.reasoning: Observed logical H1/H2/H3 headings and landmark regions for palette, controls, canvas, artifact, timeline, and saved workflows. Evidence: accessibility-initial.png

criteria.name: keyboard_node_first_class
criteria.description: Node selection can be cycled from the keyboard with a visible highlight; Enter opens the selected node's configuration; Delete removes the selected node, edge, or multi-selection
criteria.reasoning: Alt+N visibly selected a node, Enter opened its configuration dialog, and Delete removed the selected node with confirmation feedback. Evidence: keyboard-node.png

criteria.name: status_not_color_alone
criteria.description: Every status badge and the graph-validity badge carries a text label; status is never conveyed by color alone
criteria.reasoning: Graph validity and execution states were presented with text labels such as Valid, Incomplete, Ready, complete, and failed rather than color alone. Evidence: accessibility-initial.png

criteria.name: focus_indicators_visible
criteria.description: Focused controls show a visible focus ring that is not obscured by overlapping chrome
criteria.reasoning: A focused palette control visibly exposed a 2px blue outline and inset focus ring, unobscured by surrounding chrome. Evidence: focus-visible.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_still_operable
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, all flows — run, undo, import, export — remain completable with keyboard and pointer
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: With prefers-reduced-motion confirmed true, run, artifact, and import were operable, but the full required set including undo was not completed; Undo remained disabled in the tested state. Evidence: reduced-motion-import.png

criteria.name: multi_facet_reload_resets_to_seed
criteria.description: Starting from seed: add a node, save a configuration, save a named workflow, open Artifact, run once, undo once, then reload. Confirm every facet coherently resets — seeded 5-node graph, empty timeline, no saved-run state, empty undo/redo, artifact previews matching the seed — never a partial mix
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Reload returned the seeded 5-node graph, but the complete add/save/workflow/artifact/run/undo sequence was not successfully verified as one coherent workflow. Evidence: reload-seed.png

criteria.name: timeline_filter_reversal_proves_live
criteria.description: Filter-reversal proof: after a run with mixed statuses, filter timeline to failed (narrowed list), then clear/all — the full ordered log returns with the same entries, proving the filter derives from live timeline data rather than two hardcoded lists
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The Failed filter produced “No failed events” rather than a narrowed failed-event list before clearing. Evidence: timeline-mixed.png

criteria.name: config_edit_derived_artifact_sensitivity
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: change an Agent timeout_seconds or a node title and confirm both Workflow Definition JSON and Mermaid previews change meaningfully to include the new values rather than redrawing identically
criteria.reasoning: Agent title/timeout edits appeared in JSON, and the Mermaid preview changed to include the edited title. Evidence: derived-sensitive.png

criteria.name: config_echoes_badge_and_export
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: save a distinctive Prompt configuration; confirm the canvas configuration badge and the JSON config.prompt field both show that same prompt name without a reload
criteria.reasoning: The canvas showed “Launch summary” and the live JSON config.prompt field showed the same value without reload. Evidence: config-echo.png

criteria.name: palette_drop_count_delta_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure visible node count, drop one palette node, re-measure immediately — count increases by exactly one; JSON nodes array length matches
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Real palette click/drag attempts left the visible node count at 5; no exact +1 drop delta was observed. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: different_timeouts_different_export
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: set Agent timeout_seconds to 5 and note JSON; set it to 120; confirm the two JSON exports differ in timeout_seconds exactly as the inputs dictate
criteria.reasoning: JSON export visibly showed timeoutSeconds 5, then after editing it showed timeoutSeconds 120. Evidence: config-export-5s.png, timeout-120-export.png

criteria.name: interleaved_run_and_export_flows
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: start a run, pause mid-run, open Artifact and confirm JSON still lists all nodes, resume to completion; confirm rollup reaches n of m complete and artifact preview was never wiped by the pause
criteria.reasoning: During a paused run, the artifact still listed all 5 nodes; after Resume the rollup reached 5/5 complete. Evidence: paused-artifact.png

criteria.name: empty_canvas_then_reseed_via_import
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete all nodes (empty-canvas run message and artifact empty-state visible), Import a previously exported valid Workflow Definition JSON; confirm nodes/edges return and both artifact formats match the import
criteria.reasoning: After deleting all nodes, the canvas and artifact showed empty states; importing valid JSON restored 5 nodes, edges, JSON, and Mermaid. Evidence: empty-canvas.png, import-restored.png

criteria.name: import_export_round_trip_probe
criteria.description: Export Workflow Definition JSON after distinctive title and timeout_seconds edits, delete nodes so the canvas differs, Import that JSON, and confirm canvas, badges, validity badge, and both artifact formats reconstruct the exported values without reload
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The reconstruction used a manually prepared import payload rather than the exact previously exported JSON, so exact round-trip fidelity was not fully verified. Evidence: import-restored.png

criteria.name: undo_restores_then_redo_reapplies
criteria.description: Delete a node so JSON omits it, Undo once and confirm the node and JSON entry return together, then Redo and confirm they disappear again across canvas and artifact
criteria.reasoning: Deleting the Agent reduced the canvas to 4 articles, Undo restored 5, and Redo reduced it to 4 again. Evidence: undo-redo-final.png

criteria.name: schema_field_contract_round_trip
criteria.description: Set distinctive valid contract values (title, timeout_seconds within 1–300, prompt/agent/rubric from seeds), export JSON, clear canvas, Import: confirm those exact field keys and values reappear in configuration modals and JSON preview
criteria.reasoning: Imported JSON showed the distinctive title, timeout, prompt, agent, and rubric values; configuration modals showed the corresponding values. Evidence: schema-prompt-modal.png, schema-agent-modal.png, schema-eval-modal.png

criteria.name: seeded_workflow_walkthrough
criteria.description: On load, the canvas shows one seeded example workflow of exactly 5 connected nodes — one each of Prompt, Agent, Eval, Condition, Output — joined by 4 edges forming a valid source-to-sink path, with a left palette listing all five node types
criteria.reasoning: PASS: The canvas visibly contained exactly five connected nodes—Prompt, Agent, Eval, Condition, and Output—with four edges, and the palette listed all five types. Evidence: seeded_workflow_walkthrough-shared.png

criteria.name: canvas_pan_zoom_drag
criteria.description: The canvas pans and zooms with the real pointer, and dragging a node repositions it with the new position holding while the app stays open; dragging a node type from the palette onto the canvas with a real drag creates a new node of that type at the drop position
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Zoom visibly changed the canvas, but node repositioning and palette drag-and-drop creation were not successfully observed. Evidence: canvas_pan_zoom_drag-shared.png

criteria.name: compatible_connection_creates_edge
criteria.description: Dragging from a node's output handle to a compatible input handle creates a directed edge between the two nodes that remains visible on the canvas; attempting an incompatible connection (for example output handle to output handle) shows an error toast naming the incompatibility and creates no edge (edge count unchanged)
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Compatible and incompatible handle-drag connection behaviors were not successfully exercised and observed. Evidence: seeded_workflow_walkthrough-shared.png

criteria.name: select_delete_edge_and_node
criteria.description: Clicking an edge selects it with a visibly distinct style and pressing Delete removes it; selecting a node and pressing Delete removes the node together with its attached edges in the same interaction
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Keyboard node deletion was observed, but edge selection with distinct styling and edge deletion were not verified. Evidence: keyboard_node_selection-shared.png

criteria.name: node_config_forms_field_contract
criteria.description: Double-clicking a node opens a type-specific configuration modal enforcing the field contract: every type requires title (1–80 chars); Prompt requires prompt from the seeded library (≥4); Agent requires agent from the seeded list (≥3) and timeout_seconds integer 1–300; Eval requires rubric (≥3); Condition requires expression; Output requires destination. An invalid field shows an inline message naming that exact field key with Save disabled until valid; saving updates the configuration badge and both artifact previews
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Prompt configuration opened and title validation was observed, but the complete per-type field contract, save behavior, and artifact updates were not fully verified. Evidence: node_config_forms_field_contract-shared.png

criteria.name: run_topological_progression
criteria.description: Pressing Run sets every node to a pending badge, then nodes advance one at a time in topological source-to-sink order through running (with an active indicator) to complete, each with a watchable simulated duration — never all at once
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Run showed Prompt running while the remaining four nodes were pending, then advanced through the workflow to 5/5 complete rather than starting all nodes simultaneously. Evidence: run_topological_progression-shared.png

criteria.name: retry_attempt_and_backoff_visible
criteria.description: During a run, the seeded Agent node visibly retries: a retrying badge appears with an attempt counter and a live backoff countdown of the form 'waiting Ns before retry 2 of 3' before the next attempt, then the node succeeds on its third attempt
criteria.reasoning: PASS: The Agent visibly showed retry state with a live countdown, “retry 3 of 3,” and later completed successfully on its third attempt. Evidence: retry_attempt_and_backoff_visible-shared.png

criteria.name: failed_run_stops_downstream
criteria.description: When a node exhausts its retries, it shows a failed badge with a short inline error summary, downstream nodes stay pending, and the run-level status reads failed
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The exercised seeded run succeeded with 5/5 complete and zero failures; an exhausted-retry failed run with downstream nodes pending was not observed. Evidence: failed_run_stops_downstream-shared.png

criteria.name: retry_from_failed_node_freezes_upstream
criteria.description: After a failed run, activating the Retry from failed node control resumes execution from exactly the failed node; upstream completed nodes keep their frozen outputs, timestamps, and complete badges and visibly do not re-execute
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: No failed run was produced, so retry-from-failed-node and frozen upstream execution could not be exercised. Evidence: failed_run_stops_downstream-shared.png

criteria.name: pause_resume_checkpoint
criteria.description: Pressing Pause mid-run freezes progression at a checkpoint (the current node finishes, no further node starts, the rollup stops advancing); pressing Resume continues from exactly the next node and nodes already complete never re-execute
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Pausing produced a checkpoint with upstream complete and downstream pending; Resume continued from the next node, with a resumed timeline entry and no upstream re-execution. Evidence: pause_resume_checkpoint-shared.png

criteria.name: rollup_derives_live
criteria.description: The toolbar rollup shows n of m nodes complete, elapsed duration, and failure count, and its values update live as nodes advance during a run, matching the badges actually visible on the canvas
criteria.reasoning: PASS: The rollup visibly changed from 0/5 while running to 1/5 and then 5/5, matching the node badges and showing elapsed time and failure count. Evidence: run_topological_progression-shared.png

criteria.name: node_io_summary_expandable
criteria.description: After a run, each executed node exposes an expandable input/output summary: activating the disclosure reveals the simulated input received and output produced, and outputs differ between nodes reflecting each node's own configuration
criteria.reasoning: PASS: After execution, expandable node summaries visibly showed simulated inputs and outputs, with differing content across nodes. Evidence: failed_run_stops_downstream-shared.png

criteria.name: timeline_ordered_timestamped
criteria.description: The timeline panel lists one timestamped entry per step transition (started, completed, failed, retry scheduled, paused, resumed) in order with newest appended at the end, and running the workflow twice produces two distinct sets of entries with fresh timestamps; filtering the timeline by status narrows the visible entries (clearing restores the full log) and selecting an entry highlights its node on the canvas, panning it into view when offscreen
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Ordered timestamped transitions were visible, but two distinct run sets, status filtering/clearing, and selecting an entry to highlight and pan to a node were not all verified. Evidence: failed_run_stops_downstream-shared.png

criteria.name: save_load_confirmation_round_trip
criteria.description: Saving via the Save modal with a valid name (1–80 chars) adds the workflow to the Saved Workflows panel with name and node count; clicking a saved workflow replaces the canvas only after a confirmation dialog is accepted (declining leaves the canvas untouched), and loading resets execution badges and the rollup; saving with an empty name shows an inline validation message naming the name field and nothing is added to the panel
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Empty-name validation was visible; a valid save produced a Saved Workflows entry with name and node count; loading required confirmation and reset execution badges and rollup after acceptance. Evidence: save_load_confirmation_round_trip-shared.png

criteria.name: keyboard_node_selection
criteria.description: Node selection can be cycled from the keyboard with a visible selection highlight, Enter opens the selected node's configuration modal, and Delete removes the selected node or edge — all without pointer use
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Alt+N selected a node with visible selection count, Enter opened configuration, and Delete removed the selected node and attached edges without pointer use. Evidence: keyboard_node_selection-shared.png

criteria.name: run_empty_canvas_message
criteria.description: Pressing Run after deleting all nodes shows a visible message that there is nothing to run and starts no execution; the Run control is also disabled while a run is already in progress
criteria.reasoning: PASS: After all nodes were deleted, Run displayed a visible nothing-to-run message and no execution began. Evidence: run_empty_canvas_message-shared.png

criteria.name: multi_select_bulk_delete
criteria.description: Shift-clicking (or marquee-selecting) a second node creates a multi-selection with distinct chrome and a live selected count in the toolbar; Delete selected removes every selected node and their attached edges in one interaction, dropping both node and edge counts and clearing the selection
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Shift/marquee multi-selection, selected count, and bulk deletion were not successfully exercised end-to-end. Evidence: keyboard_node_selection-shared.png

criteria.name: undo_redo_restores_canvas_and_artifacts
criteria.description: Undo reverses the most recent editing action (drop, delete, bulk delete, edge create/delete, reposition, configuration save, or successful import) restoring canvas nodes/edges, configuration badges, and both artifact previews together; Redo reapplies with the same completeness; empty stacks disable their controls; a new edit after undo clears redo
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Editing and deletion were observed, but complete undo/redo restoration of canvas, configuration, and both artifacts—including redo invalidation after a new edit—was not verified. Evidence: keyboard_node_selection-shared.png

criteria.name: artifact_center_json_and_mermaid
criteria.description: Opening the Artifact panel shows live-compiled Workflow Definition JSON and Mermaid tabs: JSON includes required keys name, nodes (id, type enum prompt|agent|eval|condition|output, title, position.x/y, type-specific config), and edges (id, source, target); Mermaid lists node and edge lines from the same graph; editing the canvas updates both previews without reload
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Artifact tabs visibly exposed live JSON and Mermaid representations; JSON showed schema envelope, node IDs/types/positions/config, and edges, while Mermaid listed matching node and edge lines. Evidence: artifact_center_json_and_mermaid-shared.png

criteria.name: artifact_copy_download_import
criteria.description: Copy writes the exact visible preview text and shows a brief confirmation; Download triggers a real file download with a format-appropriate filename; Import of a valid Workflow Definition JSON replaces the canvas and updates badges and both previews without reload; contract-violating JSON shows an inline message naming the offending field and mutates nothing
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Copy, download, and import controls were visible, but successful copy confirmation, real file download, valid import replacement, and invalid import immutability were not all observed. Evidence: artifact_center_json_and_mermaid-shared.png

criteria.name: graph_validity_badge
criteria.description: A graph-validity badge beside the artifact preview reads valid when the canvas has at least one Prompt, one Output, and a directed Prompt-to-Output path, and otherwise reads incomplete with a short reason naming what is missing — always with a text label, never color alone
criteria.reasoning: PASS: A text “Valid” badge was visible for the seeded Prompt-to-Output path, and an empty canvas visibly changed to “Incomplete” with a reason. Evidence: artifact_center_json_and_mermaid-shared.png; run_empty_canvas_message-shared.png

criteria.name: node_cards_show_identity_config_and_run_state
criteria.description: Every canvas node visibly shows its node type, display title, and a configuration badge summarizing its saved type-specific configuration; after execution touches the node, the same card also shows a text status badge naming its current execution state
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Executed cards visibly showed node type, title, configuration summary, and text execution status badges. Evidence: failed_run_stops_downstream-shared.png

criteria.name: saved_workflow_request_body_contract
criteria.description: Saving a workflow produces a browser-visible record with a trimmed name 2–80 characters, a non-empty nodes array whose entries have id, type exactly Prompt|Agent|Eval|Condition|Output, numeric position x/y, and matching config, plus edges whose source and target each reference an included node id; a disconnected graph may save, but Run visibly refuses a graph without a valid source-to-sink path
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Saving produced a visible saved-workflow record, and the visible live JSON showed the required node IDs, exact type names, numeric positions, matching configs, and edge references. Evidence: artifact_center_json_and_mermaid-shared.png

criteria.name: workflow_exports_include_envelope_and_live_graph
criteria.description: Export workflow, Download workflow.json, and Copy JSON expose schemaVersion exactly 1 and generatedAt as an ISO-8601 datetime ending in Z together with the active graph under the save-workflow field contract; Export Mermaid labels match the canvas node labels, and both formats update after node, edge, configuration, or Save mutations without reload
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The JSON envelope and Mermaid labels/download controls were visible, but actual Export/Copy/Download operations and update verification across all mutations were not completed. Evidence: workflow_exports_include_envelope_and_live_graph-shared.png

criteria.name: spacing_rhythm_consistent
criteria.description: Spacing between palette, canvas, toolbar, Artifact panel, Saved Workflows, and timeline follows a consistent rhythm with no crowded or orphaned regions
criteria.reasoning: Consistent spacing separates the palette, toolbar, canvas, timeline, and side panels without crowded regions. Evidence: layout-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_hierarchy_matches_spec
criteria.description: Typography matches the specified hierarchy: panel titles larger than node titles, which are larger than metadata and monospaced artifact text
criteria.reasoning: Panel headings are visibly larger than node titles, metadata is smaller, and artifact content uses a distinct monospace treatment. Evidence: artifact-open-shared.png

criteria.name: layout_matches_instruction_composition
criteria.description: Layout matches the instruction composition: left palette, central canvas, collapsible right Saved Workflows, toolbar above, Artifact when open, timeline below — not a collapsed single-column dump at desktop width
criteria.reasoning: At desktop width, the app shows the left palette, central canvas, toolbar above, right Saved Workflows rail, Artifact panel when open, and timeline below. Evidence: artifact-open-shared.png

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: Transitions are applied to the state changes the spec calls out (node drop, run pulse, modal enter/exit, panel slide, copy confirmation) rather than omitting them
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Run and retry state changes were exercised, but no active animation was observed on the running nodes/state; only ordinary UI transitions were present. Evidence: run-state-shared.png

criteria.reasoning: Observed left-border colors match blue Prompt, purple Agent, orange Eval, yellow Condition, and green Output in the palette. Evidence: layout-shared.png

criteria.name: status_palette_matches_spec
criteria.description: Status badge colors match the specified palette: pending gray, running blue, retrying amber, failed red, complete green
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Complete green, Running blue, Retrying amber, and Pending gray states were observed, but the specified Failed red badge was not directly observed. Evidence: run-state-shared.png

criteria.name: artifact_monospace_distinct
criteria.description: The Artifact preview monospaced block is visually distinct from the canvas chrome as specified
criteria.reasoning: The open Artifact preview is a visually distinct dark monospace block separated from the canvas chrome. Evidence: artifact-open-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_canvas_run_message
criteria.description: Pressing Run with an empty canvas (all nodes deleted) shows a visible message that there is nothing to run and starts no execution
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: After deleting all nodes, Run was disabled, so the requested empty-canvas Run interaction could not be exercised. Evidence: artifact-empty-canvas.png

criteria.name: delete_node_removes_edges
criteria.description: Deleting a node that has edges removes those edges in the same interaction; the edge count visibly drops
criteria.reasoning: Deleting prompt-1 reduced the visible edge list from four edges to three in the same state change. Evidence: delete-node-edges-drop.png

criteria.name: run_pause_resume_availability
criteria.description: The Run control is disabled while a run is already in progress; Pause is only available during a run and Resume only while paused
criteria.reasoning: During an active run, Run was disabled and Pause was available; after pausing, Resume was available and Run became enabled. Evidence: run-pause-resume-availability.png

criteria.name: save_empty_name_validation
criteria.description: Saving a workflow with an empty name shows an inline validation message naming the name field and stores nothing
criteria.reasoning: Saving with an empty name showed the inline message “Workflow name is required” and Save workflow was disabled. Evidence: save-empty-name-validation.png

criteria.name: timeline_entry_deleted_node_inert
criteria.description: Selecting a timeline entry whose node was deleted after the run shows the entry as inert (no crash, no dangling highlight)
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The attempted deletion did not visibly remove the agent node, so the required deleted-node timeline precondition was not achieved; selecting the entry itself did not crash. Evidence: run-pause-resume-availability.png

criteria.name: undo_redo_empty_stacks_disabled
criteria.description: Undo with an empty stack and Redo with an empty redo stack are disabled and produce no console errors
criteria.reasoning: On a fresh load, both Undo and Redo were disabled; console inspection reported zero errors. Evidence: undo-redo-empty.png

criteria.name: import_malformed_rejects
criteria.description: Import with malformed JSON or a payload missing required keys shows an inline message naming the problem and leaves the canvas unchanged
criteria.reasoning: Submitting “not json” showed “Invalid workflow definition: definition must be valid JSON” and the canvas remained empty/unchanged. Evidence: import-malformed-rejects.png

criteria.name: artifact_empty_canvas_state
criteria.description: Opening the Artifact panel with an empty canvas shows an empty-state line naming how to add nodes from the palette (or disables Download with a visible reason) — never a blank crash
criteria.reasoning: With an empty canvas, Artifact showed an Empty canvas state explaining how to add nodes from the palette, and Download was disabled. Evidence: artifact-empty-canvas.png

criteria.name: copy_exports_visible_text
criteria.description: Copying the export mid-edit copies exactly the text currently visible in the preview block
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: After clicking Copy JSON, clipboard readback was unavailable and did not match the visible preview text, so exact copying was not directly verified. Evidence: copy-exports-visible-text.png

criteria.name: timeout_out_of_bounds_named
criteria.description: Entering timeout_seconds outside 1–300 on an Agent node shows an inline message naming timeout_seconds and keeps Save disabled
criteria.reasoning: Entering 0 for the Agent timeout showed “timeout_seconds must be between 1 and 300” and Save configuration was disabled. Evidence: timeout-out-of-bounds-named.png

criteria.name: coachmark_first_run_tip
criteria.description: Beyond the specified flows, the workspace offers a tasteful coachmark or first-run tip pointing at palette drag, Run, and Artifact export without blocking interaction, and it dismisses permanently for the session once acknowledged
criteria.reasoning: A non-blocking First-run tip explicitly points to palette drag, Run, and Artifact export; after closing it, a reload showed the tip remained dismissed. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: minimap_or_fit_to_view
criteria.description: Optional excellence: a compact minimap or fit-to-view control helps reorient a large graph without replacing pan and zoom
criteria.reasoning: The canvas visibly provides both a Mini Map and a Fit View control alongside zoom controls. Evidence: minimap_or_fit_to_view-shared.png

criteria.name: execution_craft_beyond_baseline
criteria.description: Execution quality beyond the baseline: run progression, retry countdown, and artifact live-compile feel polished and intentional rather than abrupt stubs
criteria.reasoning: A live run visibly progressed node-by-node, showed retry attempts with a 2-second countdown, completed 5/5 nodes, and opened a structured live JSON artifact preview with workflow insight. Evidence: execution_craft_progress-shared.png; execution_craft_artifact-shared.png

criteria.name: thoughtful_empty_and_validity_ux
criteria.description: Empty-canvas and incomplete-graph validity messaging goes beyond a bare error — clear next-step guidance that still matches the specification
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Attempted to remove the output node through the registered action, but the visible graph and Valid state did not change, so incomplete-graph guidance could not be verified. Evidence: thoughtful_empty_and_validity_ux-invalid.png

criteria.name: keyboard_power_user_affordances
criteria.description: Power-user keyboard affordances (documented shortcuts for undo/redo, artifact toggle, or multi-select) that remain optional and never replace pointer paths
criteria.reasoning: The first-run tip visibly documents Alt+N, Ctrl+Z, and Ctrl+E shortcuts, while pointer controls remain available. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app demonstrates a significant, browser-observable excellence or inventive refinement beyond the specification that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file and would delight a real workflow author. Name the refinement and cite concrete evidence. Do not fail ordinary compliance gaps here — those belong in other dimensions.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant refinement beyond the listed criteria was directly established. Evidence: execution_craft_artifact-shared.png

criteria.reasoning: Toolbar hover visibly changed background/shadow, with a declared transition. Evidence: hover_feedback_required-shared.png

criteria.name: node_drop_scale_in
criteria.description: A node dropped onto the canvas via a real palette drag scales from roughly 0.8 to 1.0 over roughly 150 milliseconds rather than appearing instantly
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Real palette drag added a node, but it appeared with no scale animation; only a settled transform/transition was observed. Evidence: node_drop_scale_in-shared.png

criteria.name: edge_flow_progression_animates
criteria.description: During a run started from the real Run control, the currently executing node's border pulses with a repeating opacity animation until it completes or fails, and edges out of a completed node animate — a moving dash or pulse travels along the curve toward the next node — showing flow progression
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Real Run reached an incomplete-graph error, so running-node pulse and edge progression could not be exercised. Evidence: edge_flow_progression_animates-shared.png

criteria.name: badge_transitions_and_countdown
criteria.description: Status badge changes transition their color rather than hard-swapping, and the retry backoff countdown visibly ticks down once per second during the seeded Agent node's retries
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The workflow could not run because the app reported an incomplete graph, preventing retry countdown observation. Evidence: edge_flow_progression_animates-shared.png

criteria.name: timeline_entries_animate_in
criteria.description: New timeline entries animate in as they are appended during a run, and applying or clearing a timeline filter animates rows in and out rather than snapping; selecting an entry whose node is offscreen pans the canvas smoothly to the highlighted node rather than jumping instantly
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The run failed before timeline entries were created, so entry/filter/pan animations could not be exercised. Evidence: edge_flow_progression_animates-shared.png

criteria.name: modal_panel_disclosure_motion
criteria.description: Modals (configuration, save, confirmation, import) enter and exit with a short opacity/scale transition of roughly 200 to 300 milliseconds; Saved Workflows and Artifact panels slide open and closed; node I/O disclosure opens with smooth height transition and rotating chevron; incompatible-connection toasts slide in and auto-dismiss with a fade
criteria.reasoning: Configuration modal showed a 0.26s modalIn animation; Saved Workflows panel exposed a 0.25s width/transform transition. Evidence: modal_panel_disclosure_motion-shared.png

criteria.name: copy_and_bulk_delete_motion
criteria.description: Via the real Copy control, the confirmation swaps icon or label briefly and reverts; bulk-delete via Delete selected animates nodes out rather than vanishing with no transition
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Copy changed to “Copied,” but deleting the selected node removed it without observable exit animation. Evidence: copy_and_bulk_delete_motion-delete.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, node drop is instant, the running state shows a static border color instead of pulsing, edge progression uses a static highlight, panel slides are instant, and all state changes (badges, rollup, timeline, countdown text, undo, export) still occur
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Reduced-motion mode was confirmed, but the workflow could not run, so the full set of required static running/countdown/state-change behaviors could not be verified. Evidence: reduced_motion_respected-shared.png

criteria.reasoning: yes — Local cold load rendered Orchestrate Studio with interactive controls and seeded workflow UI. Evidence: cold_load_interactive-shared.png

criteria.name: console_clean_full_exercise
criteria.description: No console errors, warnings, or unhandled promise rejections appear on load or during a full exercise: canvas edits, multi-select bulk delete, connections, configuration saves, runs with retries, pause/resume, retry-from-failed, undo/redo, save/load, import/export, and timeline filtering
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Console showed no errors or warnings, but the complete prescribed exercise was not fully completed. Evidence: reload_returns_seeded_baseline-shared.png

criteria.name: state_coherence_across_surfaces
criteria.description: Node state changes reflect everywhere immediately without reload: a badge change on the canvas is matched by the rollup and a timeline entry; a configuration save updates the node badge and both artifact previews; undo/redo reverts or reapplies canvas and artifact texts together
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Run state coherently updated node badges, rollup, retry timeline, and outputs, but configuration-save artifact synchronization and undo/redo text coherence were not fully observed. Evidence: state_coherence_across_surfaces-shared.png

criteria.name: reload_returns_seeded_baseline
criteria.description: A page reload after authoring, running, saving, importing, and undoing returns the app to its seeded baseline (example workflow, empty timeline, no saved-run state, empty undo/redo, artifact previews matching the seed), with no partial mixture of old and new state
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Reload restored the seeded canvas, empty timeline, ready rollup, and no saved workflows, but the required save/import/undo workflow before reload was not completed end-to-end. Evidence: reload_returns_seeded_baseline-shared.png

criteria.name: dialog_and_live_region_semantics
criteria.description: Modal dialogs expose role dialog with aria-modal true, trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to their opener; validation messages are associated with their fields; run state transitions, copy confirmation, and import failures are announced via an aria-live region
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Observed role=dialog with aria-modal=true, live regions, Escape close, and focus return, but focus trapping and complete field-association coverage were not established. Evidence: dialog_and_live_region_semantics-shared.png

criteria.name: rapid_input_stability
criteria.description: The UI stays responsive under rapid node drags, Pause/Resume spam, rapid undo/redo, and timeline filtering mid-run — no hangs, dropped interactions, or rollup/artifact desync from canvas badges; selecting a timeline entry whose node was deleted behaves inertly
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Rapid pause/resume requests returned and the UI remained responsive, but the full required drag, undo/redo, filtering, and deleted-timeline-entry exercise was not completed. Evidence: rapid_input_stability-shared.png

criteria.name: api_shaped_schema_validation_observable
criteria.description: Forms and Import enforce the Workflow Definition field contract observably: timeout_seconds outside 1–300, empty title, or invalid node type on import each show a field-named inline error and refuse the mutation
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Timeout 0 visibly disabled Save and exposed the required 1–300 constraint; invalid import was refused with an inline error, but it reported missing generatedAt rather than invalid node type, and empty-title validation was not exercised. Evidence: api_shaped_schema_validation_observable-timeout.png

criteria.name: author_connect_run_updates_artifacts
criteria.description: Author then run: drag a Prompt node from the palette, connect it with a compatible edge, press Run — the new node advances pending→running→complete in topological order, the rollup counts it, and both Workflow Definition JSON and Mermaid previews include the new node and edge
criteria.reasoning: Observed a dragged Prompt node, a compatible edge, a completed 6/6 run, and artifact previews containing the added node and edge. Evidence: author_connect_run_updates_artifacts-shared.png

criteria.name: seeded_agent_retry_recovers
criteria.description: Watch a failure recover: press Run and observe the seeded Agent node retry with attempt counter and backoff countdown, succeed on the third attempt; the timeline shows retry-scheduled entries and the run finishes complete
criteria.reasoning: Run completed 5/5; timeline visibly showed attempts 1–3, two retry-scheduled entries with backoff, and final completion. Evidence: seeded_agent_retry_recovers-shared.png

criteria.name: retry_from_failed_preserves_upstream
criteria.description: After driving a run to a failed state (exhausted retries), Retry from failed node resumes at that node while upstream nodes keep frozen outputs, timestamps, and complete badges
criteria.reasoning: Configured Faultline Agent, observed exhausted retries and failure, then Retry from failed node showed “upstream outputs frozen” while resuming at the failed node. Evidence: retry_from_failed_preserves_upstream-shared.png

criteria.name: pause_resume_mid_run
criteria.description: Pause mid-run freezes progression and the rollup; Resume continues from exactly the next node with completed nodes untouched
criteria.reasoning: Pause produced a paused 2/5 checkpoint; Resume continued with a resumed event and completed the remaining workflow to 5/5. Evidence: pause_resume_mid_run-shared.png

criteria.name: save_load_reload_round_trip
criteria.description: Save the canvas under a new name, load a different saved workflow through confirmation, then load the first back — canvas nodes/edges and artifact previews match the saved graph each time
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Saved-workflow creation was invoked, but the complete load-different/load-first-back confirmation round trip and matching artifact previews were not observed.

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Configure a distinctive node title and Agent timeout_seconds, export Workflow Definition JSON, alter the canvas, Import the JSON — canvas, badges, validity badge, and both artifact formats reconstruct the exported graph without reload
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The complete configure/export/alter/import workflow was not exercised end-to-end in the browser.

criteria.name: undo_redo_flow
criteria.description: After a delete or configuration save that changes the artifact preview, Undo restores prior canvas and both preview texts together; Redo reapplies across those surfaces
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Delete, Undo, and Redo changed the node count as expected, but the required synchronized JSON and Mermaid preview restoration was not observed.

criteria.name: bulk_delete_then_undo_flow
criteria.description: Multi-select two nodes, Delete selected (both nodes and edges gone; JSON omits them), Undo once — both nodes and edges return together
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Multi-selecting two nodes and completing the bulk-delete/undo workflow could not be exercised.

criteria.name: reload_returns_seeded_baseline_flow
criteria.description: A page reload returns seeded example workflow, empty timeline, no saved-run state, empty undo/redo stacks, and artifact previews matching the seeded graph
criteria.reasoning: Reload visibly returned the seeded 5-node/4-edge graph, empty timeline, no saved workflows, and disabled Undo/Redo. Evidence: reload_returns_seeded_baseline_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: artifact_panel_toggle_preserves_canvas
criteria.description: Opening and closing the Artifact panel via the toolbar toggle keeps the canvas graph and run state intact; format tab switches between JSON and Mermaid without losing preview content
criteria.reasoning: Artifact panel opened with JSON, switched to Mermaid, closed, and the canvas remained 6 nodes/5 edges with the 6/6 complete run state intact. Evidence: artifact_panel_toggle_preserves_canvas-shared.png

criteria.name: dot_grid_canvas_background
criteria.description: The canvas background shows a light gray dot-grid pattern visually distinct from the surrounding panel chrome, and node tiles carry a colored left border per type — blue Prompt, purple Agent, orange Eval, yellow Condition, green Output — with the palette entries using the same per-type color coding
criteria.reasoning: Light gray dotted canvas, colored node borders, and matching palette colors are visible. Evidence: desktop-1280.png

criteria.name: edge_styling_and_selection
criteria.description: Edges render as smooth bezier curves in a mid gray, and a selected edge renders in the primary accent blue, visibly distinct from unselected edges
criteria.reasoning: Edges use curved SVG paths in gray; selecting an edge changes it to accent blue. Evidence: edge-selected.png

criteria.name: status_badge_palette_consistent
criteria.description: Status badges use one consistent palette everywhere (canvas nodes, timeline, rollup): pending neutral gray, running blue with an active indicator, retrying amber, failed red, complete green — every badge naming its state in text, never color alone
criteria.reasoning: Observed text-labeled Pending gray, Running blue, Retrying amber, Failed red, and Complete green states across nodes/timeline. Evidence: status-failed.png

criteria.name: workspace_layout_composition
criteria.description: The layout composes as left palette, central canvas, collapsible Saved Workflows panel, toolbar with Run/Pause/Resume/Retry/Undo/Redo/Save/Artifact/Delete selected (when multi-select active)/rollup, Artifact panel with format tabs and Copy/Download/Import when open, and timeline below; one consistent icon set and clear type hierarchy
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Main composition is present, including palette, canvas, saved workflows, artifact, timeline, rollup, and most toolbar actions, but Resume was not directly observed. Evidence: toolbar-active.png

criteria.name: component_states_styled
criteria.description: Buttons, selects, and inputs show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), disabled, and error treatments, with no bare browser-default-styled controls anywhere
criteria.reasoning: Observed styled hover, disabled, and invalid/error treatments with non-default controls. Evidence: workspace-desktop.png

criteria.name: multi_select_and_validity_chrome
criteria.description: Multi-selected nodes share a distinct selection chrome stronger than single-select; the Artifact preview uses a monospaced block; the graph-validity badge uses valid (green-tinted) vs incomplete (amber-tinted) treatments and always includes a text label
criteria.reasoning: Two nodes visibly share multi-selection chrome; artifact preview is monospaced; validity badge is text-labeled and amber/green styled. Evidence: multi-selected.png

criteria.name: responsive_panels_collapse
criteria.description: At 1024 pixels the Saved Workflows panel collapses behind a toggle, at 768 pixels the palette collapses and Artifact stacks below the canvas when open with timeline still reachable, and at 375 pixels no content clips or overflows and no page-level horizontal scrollbar appears while the canvas remains pannable
criteria.reasoning: At 1024 saved workflows collapses; at 768 palette collapses and artifact stacks above reachable timeline; at 375 content remains within viewport without horizontal overflow. Evidence: responsive-1024.png, responsive-768.png, responsive-375.png

criteria.name: headings_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and panel titles (Palette, Saved Workflows, Timeline, Artifact, toolbar labels), they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout
criteria.reasoning: Headings and panel titles consistently use sentence case, such as “Node palette,” “Artifact,” and “Execution timeline.” Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they use specific verbs such as Run, Retry from failed node, Delete selected, Copy, Download, and Import rather than generic Submit or OK when a specific label is possible
criteria.reasoning: Actions use specific labels including Run, Delete selected, Import, Download workflow.json, Copy JSON, and Review import. Evidence: artifact-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders validation or import errors, they name the offending field and the fix (for example timeout_seconds must be between 1 and 300), not only Invalid
criteria.reasoning: The import error identifies the problem and fix: “definition must be valid JSON” and “Fix the JSON syntax and try again.” Evidence: import-error-result-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states (Saved Workflows, timeline, Artifact preview on empty canvas), the copy explains what belongs there and the control that creates it
criteria.reasoning: Saved workflows, timeline, and empty Artifact states explain what is missing and how to proceed, including Save, Run, and Add nodes or Import. Evidence: saved-empty-shared.png

criteria.name: body_copy_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or helper copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors
criteria.reasoning: Observed body and helper copy is clear, grammatical, and free of spelling errors. Evidence: status-full-shared.png

criteria.name: no_lorem_or_todo_copy
criteria.description: Where the app renders UI copy, no placeholder lorem, TODO, asdf, or template-variable strings appear
criteria.reasoning: No lorem, TODO, asdf, or template-variable strings were observed in the rendered UI. Evidence: status-full-shared.png

criteria.name: status_labels_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders status words (pending, running, retrying, failed, complete, valid, incomplete), the same concept uses the same label across canvas, timeline, rollup, and validity badge
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The same completion concept is labeled “Complete” on canvas nodes, “complete” in the rollup, and “completed” in timeline entries, so labels are not consistent. Evidence: status-full-shared.png
